name: Cruz Verde via Robledo Loop from Villanueva
name_original: Villanueva de la Cañada, Zarzalejo, Navalagamella – ruta circular
description: This difficult 41.4-mile road cycling loop from Villanueva offers 3425 feet of elevation gain and rewarding views near Cruz Verde.
region_intro: Road cycling around Villanueva De La Cañada offers varied landscapes, from open spaces and parks to the significant Parque Regional Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama. The terrain features a mix of paved routes suitable for road bikes, with some areas including gentle ascents and descents. The region is characterized by holm oaks, riverside forests, and the Guadarrama river, providing a natural setting for cycling. This area provides a refreshing escape from urban environments, with routes catering to different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Villanueva De La Cañada The most popular road cycling route is Cruz Verde via Robledo Loop from Villanueva, a 41.4 miles (66.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and views towards the Sierra. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Escondida – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Villanueva de la Cañada, a difficult 63.7 miles (102.6 km) path. This long-distance route includes challenging climbs and offers extensive scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Valdemorillo – Peralejo Church loop from Villanueva de la Cañada, a 30.6 miles (49.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Villanueva De La Cañada is defined by open spaces, riverside forests, and rolling hills. The network offers options for easy rides and more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Villanueva De La Cañada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 10,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De La Cañada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available around Villanueva De La Cañada, catering to various fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 10,000 times.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied landscapes, from open spaces and parks to the significant Parque Regional Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama, and the refreshing escape from urban environments.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while road cycling in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: Road cycling in Villanueva De La Cañada is characterized by diverse natural features, including holm oaks, riverside forests, and the Guadarrama river. You'll encounter open spaces, parks, and rolling hills, particularly within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1629985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Regional Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt;, which boasts significant ecological value.

Q: Are there any landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by interesting landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1629985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a notable point of interest along some cycling paths. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1057168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of the Lodones&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2865476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Villaviciosa de Odón&lt;/a&gt; are also within reach.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: Villanueva De La Cañada offers a range of road cycling routes for all abilities. You can find easy routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39547846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;XXL Bicycle Sculpture loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;, as well as moderate and challenging options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977181990/cruz-verde-via-robledo-loop-from-villanueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Verde via Robledo Loop from Villanueva&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: While many routes are designed for road bikes, the municipality features a dedicated 17 km "Senda Ciclable" cycle path that winds through open spaces, parks, and gardens, which can be suitable for families. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4049634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de la Despernada&lt;/a&gt; is highlighted as an ideal spot for enjoying nature and outdoor activities with family.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Villanueva De La Cañada are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33921125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Valdemorillo – Peralejo Church loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39547846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;XXL Bicycle Sculpture loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling in the area?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31533816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Escondida – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive distances of over 100 km with challenging climbs and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villanueva De La Cañada?
A: The region's climate generally allows for cycling through much of the year. Autumn is particularly beautiful for exploring the interpretative botanical routes within the Parque Regional Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama, but spring and early summer also offer pleasant conditions.

Q: Is Villanueva De La Cañada accessible by public transport for cyclists?
A: Villanueva De La Cañada is easily accessible from Madrid city, which suggests public transport options are available. However, specific details regarding public transport that accommodates bicycles directly to the cycling route starting points are not detailed in the regional information.

Q: Are there cafes or places to refuel along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes directly on every route are not listed, Villanueva De La Cañada and surrounding towns offer amenities where cyclists can refuel. One route is even named &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Café – XXL Bicycle Sculpture loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;, indicating nearby refreshment options.

Q: Where can I find easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Beginners can enjoy several easy road cycling routes in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39547846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;XXL Bicycle Sculpture loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; is a good option, covering about 32 km with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for those new to road cycling.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can tackle routes with significant elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977181990/cruz-verde-via-robledo-loop-from-villanueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz Verde via Robledo Loop from Villanueva&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31533816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Escondida – Cruz Verde Pass loop from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; are both rated as difficult, featuring demanding climbs and longer distances.
tips: Peralejo is a town of restaurants and cafeterias where you can rest for a while and recharge your batteries. It also has the Cañada Real Nature Center, where wild animals with injuries are treated. In the surrounding area there are birds of prey, day and night, eagles, vultures, wolves, etc.
Navalagamella is known for its hiking trails and natural surroundings, perfect for outdoor activities. In addition, its historical and cultural heritage offers an enriching experience for visitors.

Quijorna is a small town nestled between fields and hills and surrounded by mountains. Here there is much peace and serenity. You will see many hundreds of rabbits living throughout the area and flocks of sheep in the fields.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike loop?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike loop, covering approximately 66.6 kilometers with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 3 hours and 21 minutes to complete. Your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult due to its significant elevation gain of over 1000 meters. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists or those looking for a demanding climb and a substantial workout.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Cruz Verde via Robledo Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact loop are not provided, Villanueva de la Cañada is the starting point. You can typically find public parking within the town center or residential areas, keeping in mind local regulations.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The Cruz Verde via Robledo Loop is predominantly paved, as is typical for a racebike route with such a substantial ascent. You can expect well-maintained roads suitable for road cycling, with challenging climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What landmarks or points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several interesting spots. These include the Peralejo Church, the historic El Pasadero Medieval Bridge and Stone Cross in Navalagamella, and the Quijorna Town Center and 1937 Bombing Memorial.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in the Madrid region, making the significant climbs more challenging. Winters can be cold, especially at higher elevations like Cruz Verde.

faqs.question: Is the Cruz Verde via Robledo Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a demanding road cycling route, it is generally not suitable for dogs. The focus is on paved roads and significant climbs, which are not ideal for canine companions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Sierra Oeste de Madrid area?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and established routes within the Sierra Oeste de Madrid area, including this loop. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several official trails for portions of its length. Notable sections include parts of the CIMA MA01 Cruz Verde - La Paradilla - Robledondo, De Colmenarejo a Valdemorillo, and the GR10, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on the Cruz Verde via Robledo Loop?
faqs.answer: As you gain elevation, especially towards Cruz Verde, you can expect rewarding scenic views of the surrounding countryside. The Madrid region offers varied landscapes, and from higher points, you'll get expansive vistas of the Sierra Oeste.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns and villages like Villanueva de la Cañada, Navalagamella, and Quijorna, you will find opportunities to stop for food, coffee, or drinks. It's always a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially on longer climbs.
